steps.title: Spot the paradox
steps.content: Males prefer green females for mating, yet 28% of females remain blue. If sexual selection alone drove coloration, blue should nearly disappear.

steps.title: Identify the non-mating driver
steps.content:
Blue must be maintained by factors other than mating selection.

Males show less antagonism toward blue in resource competition, creating a survival advantage that explains blue's persistence despite male mate preference for green.

B captures this: blue is driven by non-mating factors. A predicts the opposite (blue would decline under mate preference).

C makes an unrelated claim about perception. D restates antagonism without explaining blue's persistence.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
